message:
build(frankenterm): honor SOURCE_DATE_EPOCH in build.rs (wa-nu4.3.9.16)

release.yml:65 sets SOURCE_DATE_EPOCH="0" for reproducible builds, but
crates/frankenterm/build.rs ignored it — FT_BUILD_TS came from wall clock
via `date -u`, so the same commit built twice produced different binaries.
FT_GIT_DIRTY similarly reacted to unrelated untracked files in the
working tree.

When SOURCE_DATE_EPOCH is set:
- FT_BUILD_TS is derived from the epoch via `date -u -d @<epoch>` (GNU)
  or `date -u -r <epoch>` (BSD/macOS), with a 4-digit-year sanity check
  to reject garbage from unsupported flags, and an `epoch:<n>` fallback
  if both invocations fail.
- FT_GIT_DIRTY is forced empty (a fresh checkout with stray untracked
  files can't force a "dirty" marker into the version string).

Added cargo:rerun-if-env-changed=SOURCE_DATE_EPOCH so cargo re-runs the
script when the env var toggles.

Preserves the existing YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SSZ format so env!("FT_BUILD_TS")
consumers in main.rs and the version tests at main.rs:53666-53704 are
unaffected. No new deps.

Verified:
- rustc --edition 2024 standalone parse: exit 0
- runtime test on macOS: "0"→1970-01-01T00:00:00Z, "1700000000"→
  2023-11-14T22:13:20Z, "abc"→epoch:abc

Follow-up for CI: wa-nu4.3.9.16 acceptance includes a two-build diff
check; that's a separate workflow change.

Agent: RusticMeadow

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -118,7 +118,7 @@
 {"id":"ft-b5rbd","title":"[HIGH] GUI resize increment saturation can overflow backend min-size arithmetic","description":"Commit 193a9ebb changes `ResizeIncrementCalculator` to saturate `base_width`/`base_height` at the u16 ceiling (`crates/frankenterm-gui/src/resize_increment_calculator.rs:15-35`). Multiple window backends then immediately compute `base + increment` in u16 without guarding overflow (`frankenterm/window/src/os/macos/window.rs:1410-1412`, `frankenterm/window/src/os/x11/window.rs:1892-1893`, `frankenterm/window/src/os/wayland/window.rs:915-916`). With large padding/border/tab-bar geometry, the new saturation can produce `base_width == u16::MAX`, so `base_width + x` overflows before min-size clamping. This turns an intended overflow hardening change into a runtime panic/wraparound path on oversized layouts. Expected fix: clamp stored base dimensions to `u16::MAX - increment` (or widen backend arithmetic) and add a regression test that asserts `base + increment` stays representable.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-20T05:58:04.554054Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-20T06:42:08.945504Z","closed_at":"2026-04-20T06:42:08.945232Z","close_reason":"dup of ft-htf0i","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"ft-b677n","title":"[MEDIUM] wgpu readback poll loop has no test coverage for timeout, disconnect, or error-propagation paths","description":"Commit 6f41130c (br-43bbl fix wgpu readback polling) rewrites the
readback wait in crates/frankenterm-gui/src/termwindow/webgpu.rs:168-193
from:

\`\`\`rust
self.device.poll(PollType::Wait { submission_index: None, timeout: Some(WEBGPU_READBACK_TIMEOUT) })?;
receiver.recv_timeout(WEBGPU_READBACK_TIMEOUT)?...
\`\`\`

to a bounded poll loop:

\`\`\`rust
let deadline = Instant::now() + WEBGPU_READBACK_TIMEOUT;
loop {
    self.device.poll(wgpu::PollType::Poll)?;
    match receiver.recv_timeout(Duration::from_millis(10)) {
        Ok(result) => { result?; break; }
        Err(RecvTimeoutError::Timeout) if Instant::now() < deadline => continue,
        Err(RecvTimeoutError::Timeout) => return Err(...),
        Err(RecvTimeoutError::Disconnected) => return Err(...),
    }
}
\`\`\`

Concerns with the new implementation:

1. \`PollType::Poll\` is non-blocking by contract in wgpu 25. The loop
   relies on \`Poll\` actually driving the \`map_async\` callback forward.
   If a future wgpu point release changes \`Poll\` to be strictly
   \"dispatch-only\" (no callback invocation), the loop would always
   hit the 5s timeout. There is no test pinning this behavior.

2. The \`Disconnected\` branch is reachable only if the \`map_async\`
   closure drops without sending — that happens e.g., if wgpu drops
   the submission without invoking the callback (device lost, internal
   error). No test exercises this path.

3. \`Instant::now() + WEBGPU_READBACK_TIMEOUT\` panics on overflow
   (impossible in practice for 5s, but worth noting as a latent
   concern). Prefer \`checked_add\`.

4. Busy-wait cost: on the timeout path, the loop spins ~500 iterations
   (5000ms / 10ms) calling \`device.poll\` each time. The prior
   \`PollType::Wait\` blocked in the driver. This is a CPU-efficiency
   regression on cancellation/device-loss paths, not a correctness
   regression.

Impact: readback behavior on wgpu failure paths (device lost, long
delay, map_async disconnect) is silently unverified. A correctness
regression in \`PollType::Poll\` semantics between wgpu versions would
silently break screenshots and readback-based features.

Fix direction:
- Add a unit-level test with a mock/stub Device that controls
  \`poll()\` return timing and can force a Disconnected on the mpsc
  channel. Assert each of the four loop outcomes.
- Consider moving the readback loop into a small helper function
  that takes a \`Fn()\` poll closure + channel receiver, to make it
  unit-testable without a real GPU.
- If unit isolation is impractical, at minimum add an integration
  test that triggers a readback and asserts completion within a
  tight CPU budget (regression test for busy-wait blow-up).

Severity: medium — latent risk in a GPU-facing path that typically
works and has no automated coverage for failure modes.

Evidence: commit 6f41130c diff;
crates/frankenterm-gui/src/termwindow/webgpu.rs:168-193 at HEAD.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-20T07:25:33.419495Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-20T08:48:13.062749Z","closed_at":"2026-04-20T08:48:13.062383Z","close_reason":"Added timeout/disconnect/cancel-style readback coverage and hardened the helper deadline with checked_add","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["gpu","review","test-gap","testing","webgpu"]}
 {"id":"ft-bs0ec","title":"robot_types agent inventory golden contract","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","assignee":"pane8","created_at":"2026-04-22T04:22:08.228201Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-22T09:56:43.693909Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T09:56:43.693614Z","close_reason":"Landed in 97ec6350 (test(frankenterm-core): freeze agent_inventory_contract.json golden for robot payload)","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
-{"id":"ft-btg0h","title":"[fuzz] add frankenterm/term Terminal::advance_bytes target for full escape-to-state pipeline","description":"Surface: frankenterm/term/src/terminal.rs:164 — pub fn advance_bytes<B: AsRef<[u8]>>(&mut self, bytes: B). Master terminal state-machine entry point: raw output bytes → frankenterm_escape_parser → Performer::perform (terminalstate/performer.rs:252) → every CSI/SGR/OSC/DCS dispatch path (perform_csi_mode/window/edit/cursor/sgr in terminalstate/mod.rs:1425-2648, plus image pipelines in terminalstate/sixel.rs, terminalstate/kitty.rs (37KB), terminalstate/iterm.rs). Existing escape_parser_raw fuzz target covers only the lexer stage (bytes → Action); termwiz_csi_parser covers the escape-parser CSI roundtrip property. Nothing exercises the state-machine mutations, cursor/edit bounds, or the Sixel/Kitty/iTerm2 image decoders under arbitrary input.

Proposed harness: fuzz/fuzz_targets/term_advance_bytes.rs — construct a Terminal with a minimal TerminalConfiguration impl, 24x80 size, cap input at 256 KB, feed the whole slice through advance_bytes, drop the terminal. No assertions — libFuzzer asserts no-panic / no-OOM / no-unbounded-alloc.

Corpus seeds: plain text, ESC[H clear, SGR sequences, DECSET/DECRST modes, DECRQM, OSC 52 clipboard, OSC 8 hyperlinks, bracketed paste, CSI u keyboard, DCS sixel fragment (\\x1bPq#0;2;100;0;0#0~~\\x1b\\\\), kitty APC image fragment, iTerm2 OSC 1337 file upload preamble, overlong / truncated / interleaved-with-text variants.

Target must survive 256 KB input without: panic, unbounded allocation (scrollback bounded at 64), unterminated DCS hang, arithmetic overflow in cursor arithmetic, OOB cell index. This discovers sixel/kitty image-decoder arithmetic bugs and any CSI dispatch path that does not clamp its operand to grid bounds.

Add to fuzz/Cargo.toml: frankenterm-term = { path = "../frankenterm/term" }. No new features required — Terminal::advance_bytes is in the crate public API for terminal hosts.

Do NOT duplicate: ft-t4n0z (termwiz InputParser, closed), ft-he0w7 (native_events, closed), ft-p2bjm (replay, in flight), escape_parser_raw (lexer stage only), termwiz_csi_parser (CSI roundtrip property on escape-parser crate, not term::Terminal).","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-04-23T07:08:20.022220Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-23T17:46:43.386901Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["fuzz","security"]}
+{"id":"ft-btg0h","title":"[fuzz] add frankenterm/term Terminal::advance_bytes target for full escape-to-state pipeline","description":"Surface: frankenterm/term/src/terminal.rs:164 — pub fn advance_bytes<B: AsRef<[u8]>>(&mut self, bytes: B). Master terminal state-machine entry point: raw output bytes → frankenterm_escape_parser → Performer::perform (terminalstate/performer.rs:252) → every CSI/SGR/OSC/DCS dispatch path (perform_csi_mode/window/edit/cursor/sgr in terminalstate/mod.rs:1425-2648, plus image pipelines in terminalstate/sixel.rs, terminalstate/kitty.rs (37KB), terminalstate/iterm.rs). Existing escape_parser_raw fuzz target covers only the lexer stage (bytes → Action); termwiz_csi_parser covers the escape-parser CSI roundtrip property. Nothing exercises the state-machine mutations, cursor/edit bounds, or the Sixel/Kitty/iTerm2 image decoders under arbitrary input.

Proposed harness: fuzz/fuzz_targets/term_advance_bytes.rs — construct a Terminal with a minimal TerminalConfiguration impl, 24x80 size, cap input at 256 KB, feed the whole slice through advance_bytes, drop the terminal. No assertions — libFuzzer asserts no-panic / no-OOM / no-unbounded-alloc.

Corpus seeds: plain text, ESC[H clear, SGR sequences, DECSET/DECRST modes, DECRQM, OSC 52 clipboard, OSC 8 hyperlinks, bracketed paste, CSI u keyboard, DCS sixel fragment (\\x1bPq#0;2;100;0;0#0~~\\x1b\\\\), kitty APC image fragment, iTerm2 OSC 1337 file upload preamble, overlong / truncated / interleaved-with-text variants.

Target must survive 256 KB input without: panic, unbounded allocation (scrollback bounded at 64), unterminated DCS hang, arithmetic overflow in cursor arithmetic, OOB cell index. This discovers sixel/kitty image-decoder arithmetic bugs and any CSI dispatch path that does not clamp its operand to grid bounds.

Add to fuzz/Cargo.toml: frankenterm-term = { path = "../frankenterm/term" }. No new features required — Terminal::advance_bytes is in the crate public API for terminal hosts.

Do NOT duplicate: ft-t4n0z (termwiz InputParser, closed), ft-he0w7 (native_events, closed), ft-p2bjm (replay, in flight), escape_parser_raw (lexer stage only), termwiz_csi_parser (CSI roundtrip property on escape-parser crate, not term::Terminal).","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-04-23T07:08:20.022220Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-23T18:20:12.511809Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["fuzz","security"],"comments":[{"id":3618,"issue_id":"ft-btg0h","author":"jemanuel","text":"Implemented local diff for term_advance_bytes fuzz harness in fuzz/Cargo.toml, fuzz/fuzz_targets/term_advance_bytes.rs, and fuzz/corpus/term_advance_bytes/*.

Build blocker on verification lane:
1. rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/ft-DarkAnchor-target CC=/opt/homebrew/opt/llvm/bin/clang CXX=/opt/homebrew/opt/llvm/bin/clang++ cargo +nightly fuzz build term_advance_bytes
2. rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/ft-DarkAnchor-target CC=/opt/homebrew/opt/llvm/bin/clang CXX=/opt/homebrew/opt/llvm/bin/clang++ CARGO_TARGET_AARCH64_APPLE_DARWIN_LINKER=/opt/homebrew/opt/llvm/bin/clang++ cargo +nightly fuzz build term_advance_bytes

Both runs reach the final link for bin term_advance_bytes and fail with the same libfuzzer/c++ symbol error:
Undefined symbols for architecture arm64:
  std::__1::__hash_memory(void const*, unsigned long)
from liblibfuzzer_sys ... FuzzerLoop.o

The second run proves the explicit clang++ linker override does not fix it. Keeping bead open pending toolchain/libfuzzer resolution or alternate verified build lane.","created_at":"2026-04-23T18:20:12Z"}]}

 {"id":"ft-bx4le","title":"[bug] SignalDeduplicator silently behaves like capacity=1 when dedup_capacity=0 (connector_inbound_bridge)","description":"# Bug

connector_inbound_bridge.rs:338 SignalDeduplicator::check_and_record:

    if self.entries.len() >= self.capacity {
        self.entries.pop_front();
    }
    self.entries.push_back(...);

With `dedup_capacity = 0`:
  1. Entry 1: len=0 >= 0 true → pop_front (no-op on empty) → push_back → entries=[a]
  2. Entry 2: len=1 >= 0 true → pop_front removes [a] → push_back → entries=[b]
  3. ...each call mutates entries but len stays at 1.

Net: dedup silently operates as if capacity=1 (one-slot cache that
replaces on every call), not as "dedup disabled."

# Semantic gap

The `dedup_capacity: usize` config field advertises no special
value. Operator setting `dedup_capacity = 0` in their ft.toml
reasonably expects EITHER:
  - dedup disabled (every signal treated as new)
  - config rejected at load

Instead: dedup cache degrades to a 1-entry replacer. Every signal
is compared against only the immediately-preceding signal. Duplicate
detection is 99.99% broken, but no warning fires.

# Parallel precedent

1ad5c8cc fixed the EXACT same class in ConnectorOutboundBridge:
  `dispatch_queue_capacity == 0` now fails-fast with a distinct
  telemetry label and blocked-action record.

The inbound dedup analog needs the same treatment — short-circuit
on `capacity == 0` and bypass the dedup check, returning `true`
unconditionally (every signal is "new") so the bridge doesn't
silently corrupt its dedup semantics.

# Fix

    pub fn check_and_record(&mut self, correlation_id: &str, now_ms: u64) -> bool {
        // [ft-*] When capacity=0, dedup is effectively disabled —
        // skip all tracking and treat every signal as new. Without
        // this, the `len >= 0` check below degrades the cache to
        // a 1-slot replacer rather than honoring the "no dedup"
        // intent.
        if self.capacity == 0 {
            return true;
        }
        self.evict_expired(now_ms);
        // ... existing logic ...
    }

One test: `signal_deduplicator_zero_capacity_bypasses_dedup` —
push N entries with same correlation_id, assert all return `true`
and `len() == 0`.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-22T11:33:16.059276Z","created_by":"pane3-inbound-zero","updated_at":"2026-04-22T11:42:16.478614Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T11:42:16.478198Z","close_reason":"Landed in 8fdfd114 (fix: six independent hardening fixes … connector_inbound_bridge zero-capacity dedup bypass). SignalDeduplicator::check_and_record now short-circuits with true when capacity=0, preserving the 'dedup disabled' semantic operators expect. Companion regression test dedup_zero_capacity_bypasses_dedup pins 5-call same-id + multi-id paths. Verified: 1 passed / 0 failed / 22001 filtered (cargo test --lib --features asupersync-runtime -- dedup_zero_capacity), EXIT=0. Parallel to 1ad5c8cc outbound zero-capacity fix.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["connector","inbound-bridge","zero-capacity"]}
@@ -1269,8 +1269,8 @@
 {"id":"wa-nu4.3.9.13","title":"CI: verify fmt --check + clippy -D warnings run on BOTH ubuntu-latest and macos-latest","description":"## Gap to verify

wa-nu4.3.9 acceptance requires `cargo fmt --check` and `cargo clippy -- -D warnings` to pass in CI on BOTH macOS and Linux — platform-conditional `#[cfg(target_os)]` code can regress silently if only one platform is gated.

## Deliverable

1. Read `.github/workflows/ci.yml` and identify every job that runs fmt or clippy.
2. Confirm the `os` matrix for each covers both an Ubuntu runner AND a macOS runner.
3. If already satisfied, close as-verified with the file:line evidence in the close reason (no CI changes).
4. If a gap exists, add the missing matrix entry.

## Acceptance

- Either (a) ci.yml diff that brings it into compliance, merged, OR (b) close reason citing specific file:line ranges showing fmt+clippy already run on both platforms.
- No `continue-on-error` on the lint gates.
","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jemanuel","created_at":"2026-04-23T17:58:29.250656Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-23T17:58:58.898149Z","closed_at":"2026-04-23T17:58:58.897573Z","close_reason":"AS-VERIFIED, no diff needed. .github/workflows/ci.yml:22-76 lint job runs matrix.os=[ubuntu-latest, macos-14] (line 29) with fail-fast:false (line 27). fmt gate: cargo fmt --all -- --check at line 48. Clippy gates: default features at line 54 (cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings -D clippy::all), plus optional features tui,ftui,rollout,mcp,browser,web,metrics,distributed at line 60 with the same -D warnings flag. No continue-on-error. macos-14 is the pinned Apple Silicon runner that macos-latest currently aliases to; pinning prevents silent drift when GitHub retargets the alias. Both platforms exercised for both gates.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["ci","release","v0-1-0"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"wa-nu4.3.9.13","depends_on_id":"wa-nu4.3.9","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-23T17:58:29.250656Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"wa-nu4.3.9.14","title":"CI matrix audit: fmt/clippy/test on ubuntu-latest + macos-14 (as-verified)","description":"## Purpose

Audit `.github/workflows/ci.yml` (and siblings) against wa-nu4.3.9's DoD:

(a) `cargo fmt --check` + `cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings` + `cargo test --workspace` on BOTH ubuntu-latest and macos-latest.
(b) Failure artifact upload on every gate.

Verification only — no diff unless a gap is found.

## Acceptance

- Close reason cites the file:line evidence for each gate on each platform.
- Any found gap is fixed in a follow-up commit on the same bead.
","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jemanuel","created_at":"2026-04-23T18:10:11.358990Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-23T18:10:25.361787Z","closed_at":"2026-04-23T18:10:25.361543Z","close_reason":"AS-VERIFIED, no CI diff needed.

(a) Gate coverage on both platforms:

- Lint job (.github/workflows/ci.yml:22-76): matrix.os=[ubuntu-latest, macos-14] at L29, fail-fast:false at L27.
  * cargo fmt --all -- --check (L48) — runs on both
  * cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings -D clippy::all (L54, default features) — both
  * same clippy with --features tui,ftui,rollout,mcp,browser,web,metrics,distributed (L60) — both

- Test job (.github/workflows/ci.yml:120-150): matrix.os=[ubuntu-latest, macos-14, windows-latest] at L127, fail-fast:false.
  * cargo test --workspace --verbose (L136) — both (plus windows bonus)
  * cargo test --workspace --doc (L139) — both

macos-14 is the pinned Apple Silicon runner that macos-latest currently aliases to; pinning prevents silent drift when GitHub retargets the alias.

(b) Failure artifact uploads: present on every gate.
  * Lint: if: failure() upload lint-logs-{os} at L66-76
  * Test: if: failure() upload test-logs-{os} at L141-150
  * Feature matrix: if: failure() upload at L207-219
  * Runtime matrix: if: failure() upload at L261-272
  * Feature vendored: if: failure() upload at L295-306
  * FTUI tests, benchmarks, resize gates, frankensqlite, recorder validation, E2E approvals all use stronger if: always() uploads (L346, L404, L437, L465, L493, L633)

Siblings surveyed:
- cargo-install-validation.yml: ubuntu-latest + macos-14 matrix
- release.yml: 5-platform build matrix (not a lint/test gate)
- replay-gates / finish-line-guards / nightly-recorder / notify-acfs: ubuntu-only, specialized gates not in DoD scope

Conclusion: ship-readiness DoD for CI matrix + artifacts is fully satisfied.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["audit","ci","release","v0-1-0"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"wa-nu4.3.9.14","depends_on_id":"wa-nu4.3.9","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-23T18:10:11.358990Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"wa-nu4.3.9.15","title":"Release workflow audit: targets + reproducible flags + SHA256 + signing vs AGENTS.md Release Profile","description":"## Purpose

Audit `.github/workflows/release.yml` against wa-nu4.3.9 ship-readiness criteria:

1. Binaries built for x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu, aarch64-apple-darwin, x86_64-apple-darwin (minimum per epic body).
2. Reproducible flags: `SOURCE_DATE_EPOCH` + cargo release profile matching AGENTS.md §Release Profile (L136-147): opt-level="z", lto=true, codegen-units=1, panic="abort", strip=true.
3. SHA256 checksums published per asset + combined.
4. "Signed releases per AGENTS.md" — verify whether AGENTS.md actually mandates signing; if not, call that out; if so, cite the clause.

## Acceptance

- Close reason cites file:line evidence for each of the four checks.
- Any partial / missing coverage becomes a concrete follow-up bead with a concrete fix path.
","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jemanuel","created_at":"2026-04-23T18:17:10.536449Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-23T18:18:20.142527Z","closed_at":"2026-04-23T18:18:20.142177Z","close_reason":"AS-VERIFIED for 3/4 criteria; 2 surfaced gaps captured in follow-up beads.

1. TARGETS ✓ — release.yml:32-51 matrix:
   * x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu at L33 (required)
   * aarch64-unknown-linux-gnu at L37 (bonus)
   * x86_64-apple-darwin at L41 (required)
   * aarch64-apple-darwin at L45 (required)
   * x86_64-pc-windows-msvc at L49 (bonus)
   fail-fast:false at L29.

2. CARGO RELEASE PROFILE ✓ — Cargo.toml:547-552 matches AGENTS.md §Release Profile (L136-147) line-for-line: opt-level='z', lto=true, codegen-units=1, panic='abort', strip=true.

3. REPRODUCIBILITY PARTIAL — release.yml:65 sets SOURCE_DATE_EPOCH='0', but crates/frankenterm/build.rs:32-39 reads wall clock via 'date -u' and ignores the env var; FT_GIT_DIRTY at build.rs:17-29 depends on working-tree state. → Filed wa-nu4.3.9.16 (bug, P2) with concrete fix path.

4. SHA256 CHECKSUMS ✓ — per-asset: shasum -a 256 for unix (release.yml:89) + powershell Get-FileHash for windows (L98-99); combined SHA256SUMS manifest at L136.

5. SIGNING — AGENTS.md has zero mentions of sign/gpg/cosign/sigstore/provenance/attestation/slsa; §Release Profile does NOT mandate signing, so 'signed releases per AGENTS.md' is vacuously satisfied. release.yml has no signing either. Filed wa-nu4.3.9.17 (task, P3) as a ship-readiness follow-up with a concrete SLSA build provenance patch path (actions/attest-build-provenance@v2).

Conclusion: release.yml meets AGENTS.md §Release Profile DoD. Reproducibility has a real gap (w/ build.rs) that needs a code-scope agent; signing is a nice-to-have not in DoD.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["audit","ci","distribution","release","v0-1-0"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"wa-nu4.3.9.15","depends_on_id":"wa-nu4.3.9","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-23T18:17:10.536449Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
-{"id":"wa-nu4.3.9.16","title":"release build.rs: honor SOURCE_DATE_EPOCH for FT_BUILD_TS / FT_GIT_DIRTY so release.yml reproducibility env actually works","description":"## Bug

release.yml:65 sets `SOURCE_DATE_EPOCH: "0"` to signal reproducible builds, but crates/frankenterm/build.rs:32-39 calls `date -u +%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ` which reads wall-clock and ignores the env var. Same commit built on two different days produces different `FT_BUILD_TS` → different binary hashes → SHA256SUMS diverge for identical source state.

`FT_GIT_DIRTY` (build.rs:17-29) has a similar issue: depends on working-tree state, not commit content.

## Steps to Reproduce

1. `git checkout <tag>`
2. `SOURCE_DATE_EPOCH=0 cargo build --release -p frankenterm`
3. Wait a second. Repeat.
4. `sha256sum target/release/ft` — hashes differ.

## Fix path

In crates/frankenterm/build.rs:

- If `SOURCE_DATE_EPOCH` is set, format that epoch as UTC ISO8601 for `FT_BUILD_TS` instead of calling `date -u`.
- Either skip `git status --porcelain` entirely in reproducible mode, or gate `FT_GIT_DIRTY` on commit-level truth only.

## Acceptance Criteria

- Two back-to-back builds from the same commit with `SOURCE_DATE_EPOCH=0` produce identical `ft` binaries on the same host/toolchain.
- `ft version --full` still shows a meaningful timestamp (the SOURCE_DATE_EPOCH-derived one).

## Testing

- Add a one-shot check script or CI step that builds twice with SOURCE_DATE_EPOCH=0 and diffs the resulting binary hashes.

## Scope note

Requires editing crates/frankenterm/build.rs which is outside the current release-plumbing agent's scope — flagging for a code-scope agent. Surfaced by wa-nu4.3.9.15 release workflow audit (commit 83acd334).","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"jemanuel","created_at":"2026-04-23T18:17:52.540553Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-23T18:17:52.540553Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["build","release","reproducibility"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"wa-nu4.3.9.16","depends_on_id":"wa-nu4.3.9","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-23T18:17:52.540553Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
-{"id":"wa-nu4.3.9.17","title":"release.yml: add SLSA build provenance attestation for published artifacts","description":"## Gap

AGENTS.md §Release Profile (L136-147) does NOT currently require signing, so strictly there's no DoD miss. But the wa-nu4.3.9 mission says 'A tool that controls terminals needs high trust.' Release artifacts currently ship with SHA256 checksums (release.yml:89/99/136) but no cryptographic provenance — a user can't verify an artifact was actually produced by this repo's workflow vs tampered in transit.

## Fix path (in-scope for workflow-only agent)

Add to `.github/workflows/release.yml` `release` job:

```yaml
permissions:
  contents: write
  id-token: write        # for sigstore OIDC token
  attestations: write    # for provenance upload
```

Add a step after download-artifact, before softprops/action-gh-release:

```yaml
- name: Generate SLSA build provenance attestations
  uses: actions/attest-build-provenance@v2
  with:
    subject-path: |
      dist/*.tar.xz
      dist/*.zip
```

## Acceptance Criteria

- `gh attestation verify <artifact> --owner Dicklesworthstone` succeeds against a post-change release.
- No change to artifact naming, checksum publishing, or release notes.

## Testing

- `workflow_dispatch` the release workflow against a sacrificial tag; confirm attestations appear under the Releases page sigstore bundle and `gh attestation verify` passes.

## Why P3

Nice-to-have, not in AGENTS.md Release Profile DoD.  {"id":"wa-nu4.3.9.2","title":"Docs: README/quickstart + architecture + CLI/robot/MCP reference","description":"# Task: Documentation for v0.1

## Goal
Make the project approachable for a new contributor and operable for users.

This bead is intentionally self-contained and mirrors PLAN Appendix A + F so contributors don’t need to dig through planning docs.

## Deliverables
### 1) README (operator-first)
- What wa is (WezTerm mux “terminal hypervisor” for agent fleets).
- Quickstart:
  - install wa
  - `wa setup`
  - `wa watch`
  - `wa status`
  - `wa query` / `wa events`
- Safety guarantees:
  - observe vs act split
  - no silent gaps
  - policy-gated sending + approvals

### 2) Architecture doc
- Ingest → Storage (SQLite+FTS5) → Pattern engine → Workflow engine → Robot/MCP.
- Deterministic state via OSC 133.
- Explicit GAP semantics.

### 3) CLI reference (PLAN Appendix A)
Human-oriented commands (rich output by default via `rich_rust`):
- `wa status` — panes + inferred agent state
- `wa watch` — run daemon (foreground/background)
- `wa stop` — stop watcher in workspace safely
- `wa events` — recent/unhandled events
- `wa query` — FTS search
- `wa send` — guarded send to a pane
- `wa reserve` — reserve a pane for exclusive use
- `wa reservations` — list active reservations
- `wa approve` — grant allow-once approvals
- `wa workflow` — run a workflow manually (guarded)
- `wa audit` — audit trail of actions (redacted)
- `wa history` — action timeline + undoability
- `wa undo` — undo supported actions (with confirmation)
- `wa rules` — list/test rule packs, show matching traces
- `wa accounts` — list/refresh accounts, show rotation picks
- `wa setup` — canonical WezTerm configuration (local/remote)
- `wa doctor` — environment checks (wezterm presence, DB health)
- `wa diag bundle` — sanitized diagnostic bundle export
- `wa export` — export slices/history/audit
- `wa web` — optional HTTP server (`fastapi_rust`) if enabled
- `wa tui` — optional TUI (`charmed_rust`) if enabled
- `wa sync` — optional sync (`asupersync`) if enabled

Robot mode commands (stable JSON envelope; token-efficient):
- `wa robot state [--domain <name>] [--agent <type>]`
- `wa robot get-text <pane_id> [--tail N] [--escapes]`
- `wa robot send <pane_id> "<text>" [--no-newline] [--wait-for "<pat>"] [--timeout-secs N]`
- `wa robot wait-for <pane_id> "<pat>" [--timeout-secs N]`
- `wa robot search "<fts query>" [--pane-id <id>] [--since <iso8601>] [--limit N]`
- `wa robot events [--unhandled] [--pane-id <id>] [--type <event>] [--limit N]`
- `wa robot workflow <name> <pane_id> [--force]`
- `wa robot reserve <pane_id> --ttl <secs> --reason <text>`
- `wa robot release <pane_id>`
- `wa robot reservations`
- `wa robot accounts [--service <openai|anthropic|google>]`
- `wa robot accounts refresh [--service <openai|anthropic|google>]`
- `wa robot rules list [--pack <name>]`
- `wa robot rules test "<text>" [--agent <type>]`
- `wa robot quick-start`

### 4) MCP reference (PLAN Appendix A)
Tools (names are short and obvious):
- `wa.state`
- `wa.get_text`
- `wa.send`
- `wa.wait_for`
- `wa.search`
- `wa.events`
- `wa.workflow_run`
- `wa.accounts`
- `wa.accounts_refresh`
- `wa.rules_list`
- `wa.rules_test`
- `wa.reserve`
- `wa.release`
- `wa.reservations`

Resources:
- `wa://panes`
- `wa://events`
- `wa://accounts`
- `wa://workflows`
- `wa://rules`
- `wa://reservations`

### 5) Library integration map (PLAN Appendix F)
| Library | Role in `wa` |
|---------|--------------|
| `cass` (`/dp/coding_agent_session_search`) | Correlation + session archaeology; used in status + workflows |
| `caut` (`/dp/coding_agent_usage_tracker`) | Usage truth + selection; used in accounts + `handle_usage_limits` |
| `rich_rust` | Human-first CLI output (tables/panels/highlight) |
| `charmed_rust` | Optional interactive TUI (pane picker, event feed, transcript viewer) |
| `fastmcp_rust` | MCP tool surface for agent control (mirrors robot mode) |
| `fastapi_rust` | Optional HTTP server for dashboards/webhooks (read-only first) |
| `asupersync` | Remote bootstrap/sync layer (configs, binaries, DB snapshots) |
| `playwright` | Automate device auth flows with persistent profiles |
| `ast-grep` | Structure-aware codebase scans in “unstick agent” workflows |

## Testing / doc correctness
Docs must not drift.

- The quickstart commands shown in README should be validated regularly:
  - either by referencing existing E2E scripts, or
  - by adding a small “docs smoke” CI step that runs the exact commands from the docs in a controlled environment (tracked by `wa-nu4.3.9.9`).

- Robot mode docs should match the schema contract:
  - reference the versioned JSON schemas and keep field names consistent.

## Acceptance Criteria
- A new engineer can get to a running watcher and see events with no oral tradition.
- CLI/robot/MCP references match implemented command surfaces and schemas.

 {"id":"wa-nu4.3.9.3","title":"Version/build metadata: `wa --version` shows semver + build info + enabled features","description":"# Task: Version/build metadata (`wa --version`)

## Goal
Make it easy to know exactly what build of wa is running when debugging bugs, comparing machines, or filing issues.

This becomes non-optional once we span multiple machines and (optionally) a vendored WezTerm build.

## Output contract
- `wa --version` prints a **single, stable** line by default (human-friendly, scriptable).
- `wa --version --verbose` prints a multi-line block with detailed metadata.
- Output must not require a TTY.

## Fields to include (when available)
- `wa` semver
- build timestamp (UTC)
- git commit hash (if built from a git checkout)
- rustc version
- target triple
- enabled cargo features (mcp/web/tui/sync/browser/vendored/etc.)

Vendored mode (PLAN Appendix H):
- when `--features vendored` is enabled, include:
  - vendored WezTerm commit hash (the pinned commit)
  - any compatibility/version string we can reliably extract

## Why this matters
When automation spans multiple machines, “what version is running?” is the first diagnostic question. Vendored mode adds an additional critical dimension (which WezTerm commit?).

## Testing
- Unit tests for `--version` output:
  - non-empty
  - contains semver
  - stable (no ANSI, no random ordering)

- Verbose output tests:
  - includes the detailed fields and feature list
  - when built with vendored feature (in CI matrix), includes vendored commit hash

## Acceptance Criteria
- `wa --version` output is stable and does not require a TTY.
- `wa --version --verbose` provides enough metadata to uniquely identify the build.
- Vendored builds surface vendored commit metadata deterministically.

 {"id":"wa-nu4.3.9.4","title":"Release packaging: reproducible builds + single-binary install path","description":"# Task: Release packaging

## Goal
Provide a simple, reliable way for users to install and update wa:
- build a single lean binary
- ship artifacts with enough metadata to debug
- keep installation friction low

## Deliverables
- Release build produces a single binary (`cargo build --release`).
- Provide at least one installation path:
  - downloadable release artifact (preferred)
  - optional `cargo install --path .` for devs

- Document:
  - minimum supported OSes
  - required external deps (e.g., WezTerm installed)
  - feature flags and what they enable

## Reproducibility & safety
- Aim for reproducible builds (same inputs → same hash) where feasible.
- Publish checksums for release artifacts.
- Never require users to run unsafe install commands.

## Testing
- Smoke tests for the released binary:
  - `wa --version --verbose`
  - `wa --help`
  - `wa doctor` (in a minimal environment)

- Feature-matrix build verification in CI (tracked in CI bead):
  - default features
  - `--features mcp`
  - `--features web`
  - `--features tui`
  - `--features sync`
  - `--features browser`
  - when vendored exists: `--features vendored`

## Acceptance Criteria
- A user can install and run `wa --help` without cloning the repo (via a release artifact).
- Release artifacts include build metadata (`wa --version --verbose` is meaningful).
